ety: v.n. — or 3rd pers. sing. pret. — of burdan, 'to carry, &c.
def: s.f. Bearing, carrying; bearing or carrying off; capture, prize, acquisition, gain, profit, haul, catch; pocketings, pickings, perquisites, bribes; stake, wager, bet; (in chess) the state in which an adversary has lost all his pieces, the king alone remaining, the game so won (it is not considered so creditable as 'check-mate')
subs: adj. lit. 'Bearing a burden'; forbearing, tolerant, longsuffering, patient, gentle, mild, meek; — burd-bārī, s.f. Bearing of a burden; forbearance, toleration, longsuffering, patience, meekness, gentleness
v.t. To lose, miss, forfeit; to waste, squander
v.n. To gain a victory, win the game (at chess); to make an acquisition; make a haul, have a windfall, &c.

ety: perf. part. of burdan; rt. Zend bar = S. भर्
def: part. Borne; bearing, possessing (used in comp. e.g. nām-burda, adj. Named, having the name, &c.).
= P بردباري burd-bārī, See s.v. burd.
= P بردبار burd-bār, See s.v. burd.
adj. See s.v. burd.
adj. = burā; and buḍḍhā, q.q.v.
adj.& s.m. = buḍḍhā and būṛhā, q.q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Old, aged; — old man ( = buḍḍhā, q.v.)
subs: būṛhā-aṛhā (for būṛhā-bāṛhā, s.m. = būṛhā baṛā, q.v.
s.m. Reverend father; name of a saint
(or baṛā būṛhā), s.m. Respectable old man, elder, superior; experienced head of a family; patron
s.m. Affectation and blandishments of youth in old age
s.m. White top-knot, gray hairs; old age
būṛhā khappāṭ s.m. Decrepit old man; worn-out animal
v.n. To become old or aged.

def: s.f. = buṛhāpā, q.v.
s.m. Demonstration, proof.
s.m. Old age, senility, senile decrepitude (syn. buṛhāpā).
s.f. = buṛhaʼǒtā, q.v.
s.m. Old age, senility.
s.m. Old age, &c. = buṛhā-pā, &c. q.v.

s.m. Old man ( = būṛhā, q.v.).
s.f. Affectation of youth in old age; state of an old dotard, doting, dotage; — s.m. = buṛhbhasiyā, q.v.
s.m. One who affects in old age the manners of youth; an old dotard.
s.f. = buṛhiyā, q.v.
s.f. An old woman = buḍḍhī, q.v.
s.f. Old woman; mother; a light, soft substance (vulg. called buṛhrā).
adj. & s.m. Old, &c. (buḍḍhā, būṛhā, q.q.v.).
s.f. Point of a spear; spike at the end of a staff.
part. Cut, clipped, amputated; — s.m. Field (or fields) cut by stealth by a cultivator.
adj. Fit, or deserving, to be cut.
s.m. A diver (syn. g̠ota-ḵẖor; g̠awwāz).
s.m. Bastion, tower, turret; a sign of the zodiac; asterism, constellation
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Arabic بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Classical Syriac ܒܘܪܓܐ, borrowed from Latin burgus or Ancient Greek πύργος (púrgos).

def: s.m. Cable, large track-rope (a small one being called guṇ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Arabic بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Classical Syriac ܒܘܪܓܐ, borrowed from Latin burgus or Ancient Greek πύργος (púrgos).

def: s.f. See s.v. bur.
s.f. Small tower, turret cut-water of the pier of a bridge.

s.m. A thing with which a woman veils her face, having in it two holes for the eyes (it is a long strip of cotton or other cloth, concealing the whole of the face of the woman wearing it, except the eyes, and reaching nearly to the feet)
subs: burqe-wālī, s.f. A woman who wears a burqāʻ.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُرْقَع (burqa'), borrowed from Arabic بُرْقُع (burquʕ).

def: s.m. Clutch, gripe; snap, bite ( = buṛkī)
s.m. A small cake of cow-dung with a hole in the middle, placed on the mouth of a vessel, &c.
v.n. = buraknā, q.v.
s.f. = buṛkā, q.v.: — buṛkī mārnā = buṛkā mārnā.
s.f. Dive, dip, plunge ( = ḍubkī, q.v.)

r: burkī, per met., bukrī, bukṭī, bukṭā
ety: per met. for bukrī = bukṭī, dim. of bukṭā, q.v.
def: s.f. A pinch (as of salt, &c.); a pinch of dust used as a charm; (hence) charm, enchantment
subs: (-par), burkī mārnā, v.t. To charm, enchant, bewitch.

def: s.m. A small kind of wasp.
v.n. = būṛnā, q.v.

ety: būṛ˚ = Prk. वुड्ड(इ) = S. व्रुड(ति), rt. वुड्
def: v.n. To dip, be dipped, be immersed; to dive; to sink, be drowned ( = ḍūbnā)
subs: v.n. To die by drowning, be drowned, to drown.

def: s.m. = būṛhā-pan; buṛhāpā, qq.v.
s.m. = būṛhā-pan; buṛhāpā, qq.v.

s.m. = buḍḍhāpan and buṛhāpā, q.q.v.
s.m. Interior cavity, hollow; anything that is hollow within (as a drum, dry pumpkin, &c.); the seed of the kusūm plant; pimple, blotch, eruption (on the skin).
adj. Sharp, cutting.
s.m. = burās, būrāṅs, q.q.v.
s.m. The tree Rhododendron arboreum (common on the outer ranges of the Himalaya from the Indus to Bhūṭān; also written burrāṅs, būrāṅs.
s.f. Sowing by dropping seed into the furrow from the hand (instead of sowing broadcast or by drill).
s.f. = burrī, q.v.

ety: abst. s. from burrīdan; see burrān
def: s.f. Cutting; sharpness.
s.f. Earthen pot or bowl for fire; crucible (of a goldsmith, &c.; syn. gursī).
s.f. Coldness, coolness, chilliness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برودت (burūdat), from Arabic بُرُودَة (burūda).

def: s.m. Bastions, towers; signs of the zodiac.
s.m. Cane-weavers; basket-maker; mat-maker ( = P. boriyā-bāf).
s.m. = bhus, q.v.
v.n. To smell fetid, to stink (as rotten meat or food); to become rotten; to become sticky.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀲𑀇 (vasaï), from Sanskrit वस॑ति (vásati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwásati, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wéseti, from *h₂wes- + *-eti.

def: s.m. Coral, and its root.
s.m. Rose-hower; place abounding with sweet-smelling flowers or fruits; rose-garden.
adj. Rude, impudent, audacious, insolent, presumptuous ( = gustāḵẖ, q.v.).
s.f. Rudeness, &c. ( = gustāḵẖī, q.v.).
s.m. Flower-garden ( = būstān or bo-stan q.v.).

r: būstān, bostān, bo, stān, lit., Saʻdī
def: (bo + stān), s.m. lit. 'Place of fragrance'; flower-garden; pleasure garden; name of a celebrated work by the Persian poet and moralist Saʻdī.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بوسْتَان (bōstān). By surface analysis, بو (bo, “fragrance”) + ـسْتان (-stān, “place of”).

def: adj. Relating to a flower-garden; of the flower-garden.
s.m. Idol, image, statute; a beloved object, mistress ( = māshūq); blockhead, dolt; — adj. Stock-still, speechless, dumb; stupefied, senseless
subs: To turn into a statute, become rigid and speechless as a statue
s.m. A worshipper of images, an idolater; — but-parastī, s.f. Image-worship, idolatry
s.m. A carver of images or idols; sculptor
s.f. Sculpture, statuary; the art of a sculptor
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بت (bat, “mark, score”).

def: s.m. Hazard-table, an inclined plane along which dice or cowries are rolled (in gambling); — blow with the fist; box on the ear; cuff ( = buttā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بت (bat, “mark, score”).

def: Strength, power, ability.
s.m. Flower, sprig (particularly worked on cloth, or painted on paper); bush, shrub
subs: būṭā-sā qad, adj. Of small stature; tiny; of lovely and delicate proportion
(-par), To work flowers (on cloth), to embroider

def: s.m. Crucible, melting-pot; shrub, spreading bush (cf. būṭā).
Strength, power, ability.
s.f. A little idol.
v.t. To extinguish, put out (fire, light, &c.; syn. bujhānā).
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. Force, violence.
s.m.? A term applied to an extra cess upon a cultivator.

ety: contrac. of buyūt-āt, pl. of bait
def: s.m. Account of household expense; marketing charges (cf. buyūt).
adj. Relating to household expense.
s.f. Explaining, instructing ( = bujhāʼī).

ety: but˚ = Prk. वुत्त(इ), or वोत्त(इ), or वावत्त(इ) = S. व्याववर्त्त(ते), rt. वि+आ+वृत्
def: v.n. To go out, be extinguished (a light, &c.; — syn. bujhnā).

s.m. Pretext, evasion, shuffling; over-reaching, trickery, cajolery, fraud, deception

def: s.m. Blow, cuff, box ( = but).
s.f. Means of subsistence; provision (for a journey)
subs: To search earnestly for (anything), to go in search (of); to rummage.

def: s.f. The act of sowing, sowing; seed-time ( = boʼārā).
v.t. To cause to be sown; to have or get (a field) sown or cultivated; to get (a cow or buffalo) bulled.
Sowing, cultivation; seed-time ( = boʼāʼī; bonī).
Sowing, cultivation; seed-time ( = boʼāʼī; bonī).
s.f. pl. (of bait), Houses.
s.f. Household expenses, account of household expenses; house-taxes, &c. ( = būtāt, q.v.).

s.m. The Ibis ( = buzzā, q.v.).
s.m. Liquor made from rice, barley, or millet; beer

def: s.m. Spittle.
s.m. Young of a goat (buz); kid.
s.m. = būzna, q.v.
s.m. Monkey, ape (syn. bandar).

ety: Pehl. vazr; Zend vazra; S. vajra
def: adj. & s.m. Great, reverend, venerable, aged, noble, respected, respectable; — great man, grandee; old man, elder, respectable person; holy man, saint; sage, wise man; ancestor, forefather
subs: adj. Of noble birth, high-born; — buzurg-zādagī, s.f. High birth
adj. Aged, old, stricken in years
adj. Noble-minded, magnanimous; — buzurg-manishī, s.f. Greatness of mind, magnanimity
adj. & s.m. Great, noble, &c.; great man, noble; ancestor; sage; saint ( = buzurg)
ex: مَیں نے سُنَا ہَے کِہ جِس آدْمی کے سَاتھ میرِی شَادِی ہونے وَالِی ہَے وُہ کَافِی بُزُرْگ ہَیں
ma͠i ne sunā hai ki jis ādmī ke sāth merī śādī hone vālī hai vo kāfī buzurg ha͠i
I've heard that the man I'm getting married to is quite old.

def: s.m. pl. (of buzurg), Great men, grandees; ancestors; sages; saints; doctors.
adj. & adv. Like a great man; befitting a great man; noble, magnificent; nobly, magnificently.
s.f. Greatness, grandeur, dignity, respectability, eminence, exaltation, nobleness, excellence, superiority, high rank, seniority.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. buzurg.
s.m. Name of the famous minister of Nūshīrvān, king of Persia, who lived in the sixth century of our æra.
s.m. A species of heron, coloured black and white; the Ibis.
s.m. Hunter, sportsman, fowler; a low or wicked man.
s.m. = byādh, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Diseased, sick, ill, afflicted, pained; — an invalid; — hunter, &c. ( = byādh); — s.f. Huntress, &c. (see byādh).
s.m. Illness, sickness, ailment, disease; pain, anguish; contention, wrangle, quarrel.

adj. & s.m. Wicked, villainous, bad, vicious; cruel, fierce; — villain, cheat, rogue; an evil spirit; a snake.
adj. Forty-two.
s.m. The evening meal, supper ( = biyārī).

def: s.m. Bringing forth, act of parturition, birth; — adv. At a birth (usually with ek).
v.n. To bring forth, give birth (to, — applied to animals), to calve, foal, farrow, litter, pup, yean.
s.m. Sarcasm; covert but intelligible expression of suspicion or contempt.
s.m. = byān, q.v.
s.m. The space between the tips of the fingers of either hand when the arms are extended, a fathom.

adj. Eighty-two.
part. Pained, distressed; alarmed.
part. Passed, elapsed, &c. ( = bītā; see bītnā).
s.m. = byāhlā, q.v.
s.f. = byāhlī, q.v.

def: s.m. Trader, money-lender; creditor.
adj. & s.m. Mercantile, commercial; wonted, customary, usual; — trader, merchant; party engaged in any business or affair; money-lender.
s.m. Trader, money-lender; creditor.

subs: (-par), To be curried (leather); — is-par byoṅgā nahīṅ phirā, His hide has not been curried yet (said cf a spoiled child, &c.).

def: s.f. Dim. of byoṅgā, q.v.
s.m. The act of cutting out clothes; out, fit, shape, fashion; plan, scheme, contrivance; suitable or proper time or season, opportunity; agreement, concurrence, union; economy, frugality.
s.m. = byoṅt, q.v.
v.t. = byoṅtnā, q.v.

s.m. Traffic, trade, business, labour ( = baipār; byohār).
s.m. Dealer, trader, merchant ( = baipārī).

ety: S. व्यवहारः, rt. हृ with वि and अव
def: s.m. Difference, distinction; account, explanation, history, circumstantial or detailed account ( = tafṣīl); news, tidings, intelligence; day-book
subs: byorā karnā (-kā), To relate, describe; to inform, intimate
beʼore-wār, adj. & adv. Circumstantial, detailed, explicit, distinct, clear, plain; — circumstantially, in detail, explicitly, &c. (syn. tafṣīl-wār; biʼt-tafṣīl).

def: s.m. = byoṅt, q.v.
v.t. = byoṅtnā, q.v.
v.t. = byoṅtnā, q.v.
v.t. To have or get clothes cut out, sewn, or made up.

conj. & adv. And, both, also, moreover, as well as (usually placed, like the Latin que and the Greek τε, as an enclitic after the word or clause which it connects with what precedes); otherwise; either, or; even indeed, certainly; just; mutually, equally; but, on the contrary, yet, nevertheless; for, on account of; — adj. & s.m. Seedless; bad, vile, base, mischievous; — a thief; a tortoise; an epithet of Śiva: — ća may also be used as an expletive.
s.m. = ćāʼo, or ćāh, qq.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چَا (čā), ultimately from Chinese 茶 (chá).

r: ća, bāg̠-ća, īća, bāg̠-īća, za, īza, ja, zha, mashkīza
def: (old P. ća; Zend ća; S. क), A particle affixed to Persian nouns to form diminutives (e.g. bāg̠-ća, 'a small garden': it may also take the form -īća, as bāg̠-īća: it sometimes occurs also in the forms -za, -īza, — the letters ća, ja, za, and zha being interchangeable, — e.g. mashkīza, 'a small leathern bottle').
s.f. Tea
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چَا (čā), ultimately from Chinese 茶 (chá).

ety: rt. of ćabā-nā+āʼī = Prk. एअव्व अइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Chewing, mastication; champing, crunching.
s.f. Throbbing, throb, shoot of pain ( = ṭabhak; ṭapak, qq.v.).
v.n. To shoot with pain, to throb ( = ṭapaknā).

ety: ćabā˚ = Prk. चव्वाव(इ), or चव्वावे(इ) = S. चर्व+आपय(ति), rt. चर्व्
def: v.t. To bite (the lip); to gnaw; to champ; to crunch; — to cause to chew ( = ćabwānā)
subs: To speak with study or preparation; to speak without reserve; to mince one's words, to speak haughtily or scornfully or affectedly; to hum and haw, to drawl; to express what one has to say by little and little; to clip one's words, to mouth, to speak indistinctly.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Childish, boyish, puerile ( = ćibillā).

ety: by redupl. of Prk. चव्वडी = S. चर्व+र+ई
def: s.f. Jawing, jabber, chatter.

ety: by redupl. of Prk. चव्वडी = S. चर्व+र+ई
def: s.f. Jawing, jabber, chatter.
s.m. Something to be munched or chewed; parched gram or grain (used as food).
s.f. Parched grain ( = ćabenā); a lunch of parched grain or sweetmeats, &c., given at noon.

ety: for ćubhak, ćubha+S. कृ; — see ćubhnā
def: s.f. A prick; a sting.
s.f. & adv. = ćapaṛ-ćapaṛ, q.v.
v.t. To crunch or crush (with the teeth).
s.f. (local) A key (syn. tāl).
s.f. (local) A key (syn. tāl).

ety: i.q. ćabānā, lā taking the place of ā as caus. augment
def: v.t. To chew slowly; to eat without relish; — to mouth (one's words), to mumble.
s.f. (prov.) corr. of ćaqmāq, q.v.

ety: Prk. चव्वणअं = S. चर्वणीयं, rt. चर्व्
def: v.t. To chew, masticate; to press with the teeth, to bite; to champ; to gnaw; to crunch ( = ćabānā)
subs: ćāb-ćāb bāteṅ karnā = ćabā-ćabā-ke bāteṅ karnā

def: v.n. To be chewed or masticated.
adj. Requiring to be chewed
s.m. (dialec.) One addicted to joking, a jester.

ety: rt. of ćabānā or ćabonā+Prk.अडो and अडी (with व inserted
def: adj. Crushed, bruised; — s.f. Jesting, joking; jest, joke.
adj. & s.m. Chewing, gnawing; — chewer, &c.
adj. Quick, active, alert; — s.m. A horse-whip, a whip
subs: adj. Nimble, active, alert, expeditious; dexterous, adroit; light-fingered; beautiful
s.m. A horse-breaker; a rough-rider; a good rider, a jockey
s.f. The business of a horse-breaker, &c.; breaking-in horses; jockeyship

def: s.f. Activity, alertness, agility, celerity, despatch.

ety: ćabū˚ = ćawū = ćaʼū = S. चतुर्; cf. ćaubār
def: s.m. A terrace, a raised and levelled piece of ground (to sit and converse on), a platform; a market-place; a boundary mark; a police station; a tribunal or court of justice; a custom house
subs: To make a terrace or platform, &c.
(-ko), To convey to the police station.

ety: ćabū˚ = ćawū = ćaʼū = S. चतुर्; cf. ćaubār
def: s.m. A terrace, a raised and levelled piece of ground (to sit and converse on), a platform; a market-place; a boundary mark; a police station; a tribunal or court of justice; a custom house
subs: To make a terrace or platform, &c.
(-ko), To convey to the police station.

ety: doub. caus. of ćābnā; — wā˚ = awāw = āw+āw
def: v.t. To cause to chew, to make (one) chew, get (one) to chew.
s.m. A paternal uncle (see ćaććā or ćaćā, the more com. forms).
s.m. Paternal uncle, father's (younger) brother; — a superior; a knowing or astute fellow
subs: v.t. To give (one) a beating, to castigate
s.m. A cousin, a father's younger brother's son.

def: adj. & s.m. Whistling warbling; — a whistler.

ety: ćaćā, q.v.+nī = S. इनी or आनी
def: s.f. Father's brother's wife, aunt.

s.m. Festive song and dance, festive sport; song and dance round a pole at the holī festival; the pole round which the dance takes place at that festival; a fair or assemblage of people collected after the holī.
adj. & s.m.(?) Cultivated or ploughed for one year after having lain fallow; — land so cultivated.
s.m. = ćuććaṛ, q.v.
s.m. = ćićinḍā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Pertaining to a paternal uncle; descended from or related through a paternal uncle
subs: s.m. Son of a paternal uncle, a cousin
s.f. Daughter of a paternal uncle, a cousin.

def: s.f. A paternal aunt ( = ćaćī, q.v.)
s.f. Father's brother's wife, aunt.
s.m. A stripe, line, score.
adj. Pertaining to a ćaćā or uncle

ety: by redupl. for S. चुष्+ कार
def: v.t. To suck (particularly a dry substance from which nothing can be obtained).

ety: by redupl. for S. चुष्+ कार
def: v.t. To suck (particularly a dry substance from which nothing can be obtained).
s.m. = ćāʼo-ćoz, and ćāh-o-ćoz. qq.v.
s.m. The tree Butea frondosa; another kind of tree.
s.m. = ćarćā, q.v.
s.f. The shrub Myrsine africana (common in the outer Himalaya; the fruit is sold in the bazaars of North-West India under the name of baibrang).
v.n. To sing, whistle, warble (as birds = ćahānā, q.v.); — also = cućiyānā, ćućānā, q.v.

s.f. A sheet; a table-cloth; a covering; a coverlet; a calico mantle or wrapper (of one fold, which reaches from the head to the ankles); — a sheet (of iron, &c.); a cascade; a kind of fire-work (in imitation of a cascade); — shot and shell
subs: (-kī), To take off the covering or mantle (of a woman), to disgrace or insult (a woman)
(-par), To lay the cloth (on a table); to spread or put down a sheet
To present a ćādar or sheet as an offering (to be spread over the tomb of a saint); — ćādar-ćhat, s.f. A ceiling-cloth.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چَادَر (čādar). Doublet of چَھتْری (chatrī).

def: s.m. A small sheet or mantle (covering the head and shoulders down to the waist).
s.m. = ćaḍḍhā, q.v.

ety: P. ćādar, ćadar; prob. akin to S. छद्
def: s.f. A sheet, &c. ( = ćādar, q.v.)
subs: s.f. Throwing a sheet over the bride and bridegroom, a marriage ceremony among the Sikhs when a man marries a widow
A slight shower, sufficient to wet the ćaddar.

def: s.m. The groin; a bubo; — a buffoon, merry-andrew.
s.f. (dialec.), Riding, mounting (on an animal, or a man's back).
s.f. (dialec.), Riding, mounting (on an animal, or a man's back).
s.f. Tea ( = ćā, q.v.).
s.f. Noise (of birds, &c.), chirping; cawing; chatter, gabble, jabber.
s.m. A prop; a trellis; an arbour; a saloon built of wood and supported on columns.
s.m. A prop; a trellis; an arbour; a saloon built of wood and supported on columns.

ety: prob. for ćaptī, rt. ćap = S. चप्
def: s.f. A flatterer.
adj. (dialec.) Thick, compact, massive, heavy (cf. ćikārā)

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. S. गृ
def: v.t. To chew slowly and long, to turn round and round in the mouth; to eat without appetite or zest or pleasure.
s.m. A wall-eyed horse.
s.m. = ćakarbā, q.v.
s.m. Name of a Mog̠al family or tribe to which the house of Tīmur belongs.
s.f. = ćaṅger, q.v.
s.m. = ćakkas, q.v.

ety: S. इच्छा, rt. इष्; or rt. of ćāhnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Wish, desire, inclination; volition, will; longing, craving; love, affection, liking, fondness; fancy; choice; — appetite, relish, zest, gusto; — want, need, requirement; requisition, demand, request
subs: adv. With pleasure; with appetite or relish; eagerly; affectionately, lovingly; of one's own accord, voluntarily
(-kī), To have a desire, &c. (for); to be in request or demand.

ety: Pehl. ćāh; Zend ćāt, rt. kan = S. khan
def: s.m. A well, a pit; a dimple
subs: ćāh-ě-ẕaqan, s.m. The dimple of the chin

def: s.m. A platform; a pier-head.
s.m. A well
subs: s.m. A small reservoir, a cistern, a vat; a hole into which water descends, a sink-hole.

def: s.m. A snipe; a sand-piper.
s.m. What is desired, one's pleasure; desire, will; love, affection ( = ćāh)
subs: s.f. The love of one's country.

def: adj. Longing, desirous, craving; fond, affectionate; friendly; — s.m. A friend; a lover.
v.t. To burn, to cauterize, to blister; to brand.
v.n. To chirrup, to whistle, to sing or warble (as birds; = ćahćahānā, q.v.); — to sound (like fireworks, &c.); — s.m. Whistling, singing, &c.
s.m. A strong kind of soil (ranking between the rauslī or 'sandy' and the ḍakurā or clayey).
v.n. To be fatigued, to tire ( = ćahlā nikalnā, q.v.s.v. ćahlā).

ety: Pehl. ćihār; Zend ćathware; S. ćatvāras, m.pl. of ćatur
def: adj. Four
subs: s.m. A throne with four cushions

def: adj. Fourteen.
adj. Fourteenth.
adj. & adv. Fourth; — fourthly.

ety: rt. of ćāhnā+at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति(आपि caus. augment+ति
def: s.f. = ćāh, q.v.
s.f. = ćāhat; ćāh, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. ćah.

ety: S. चर्चरी; P. ćahćaha; prob. onomat.
def: s.m. Singing, warbling, chirping; song (of a bird); — chatter; mirth, merriment, rejoicing
subs: To sing, to warble (as birds), &c. ( = ćahćahānā, q.v.).

def: s.f. Singing, warbling (of birds), &c. ( = ćahćahā, q.v.).
v.n. To sing, warble, whistle, chirp (as birds); — to chatter gaily or merrily; to make merry, to rejoice.
s.f. contrac. of ćahćahāhaṭ, q.v.

ety: rt. of ćahćah (ānā)+Prk. इओ = S. इन्+कः
def: s.m. A warbler, a whistler.
conj. Though ( = ćāho); — either, or (usually repeated)
subs: ćāhe tau-bhī, conj. Though still or yet
However great, however much, how much soever: ćāhe jo, Whichever

def: s.m. Wet, oozy land (see ćahal and ćahlā).
s.f. A snipe, Scolopax gallinago ( = ćahī).
s.f. Warbling, &c. ( = ćahćahā, q.v. and cf. ćahaknā).
s.f. A snipe (contrac. of ćāhī, q.v.).
adj. & s.f. Irrigated from wells; — land so irrigated
s.m. = ćāhirā, q.v.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts in Hisār.
adj. & s.m. = next, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Desired, wished for; agreeable; beloved, darling; — a beloved object, a darling.
s.f. A beloved one, a sweetheart.
contrac. of ćāhiye, q.v.

r: ćāhiye, ćāhnā, opus est, necesse est, debet, oportet, ćāhiyeṅ, et seq., kyā na ćāhiye
def: (the precative form of the aorist of ćāhnā, used as a phrase), Is necessary, is needful or requisite, is proper or right; it behoves; should or ought ( = Lat. opus est, necesse est; debet; oportet; — pl. ćāhiyeṅ: see Hind. Gram. 439, et seq.)
subs: What is not wanting (to me), what do I not want, I want everything; — nothing is wanting (to me), I have everything.

def: s.m. Smarting, burning, or tingling (of the skin on the application of a medicine); a burn; a blistering plaster; a kind of firework; — a layer of stone and cement on a mud floor or roof; a pavement
subs: To burn the skin slightly (by fomenting, &c.); to apply a plaster or blister (to); — to pave.

ety: ćahk˚ = ćahuk = ćaʼuk or ćauk, q.v.+aiṭ = ait = Prk. अइत्तो+आयंतो = आंतो = S. आपयनआ (the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment āpi
def: adj. Square-built, stout, brawny, sturdy, robust.
s.f. Chirruping, singing, warbling (of birds; = ćahćahī, q.v.).
v.n. To chirrup, sing, warble ( = ćahćahānā, ćahaknā).
s.m. A splinter (of wood); — a log (of fire-wood)
s.f. The wheel on which the rope revolves at the top of a well.

ety: ćāh˚ for ćhāh˚ = Prk. उच्छाह(इ) or उच्छाह(इ), fr. S. उत्साह; or Prk. इच्छाए(इ), or इच्छाअ(इ), fr. S. इच्छा
def: v.t. To wish, desire, will; to want, demand, require, need; to be inclined to; to tend to; to be about to (with perf. part. of following verb); to intend; to like, love, be enamoured of; to choose, approve; to pray, ask for, crave, entreat, to attempt, try; — s.m. Pleasure, will, &c. = ćāhā.
v.t. To see, look at, look for; to watch, observe.
v.t. contrac. of ćāhnā, q.v.
conj. Though, even if; — ćāho ćāho, Either ... or; whether ... or (see ćāhe).
s.m. A fire sort of rice.
v.t. To transplant.

ety: imperf. part. of ćāhnā; and = ćāh˚+tā = atā = Prk. अंतओ = S. अन्त+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Owing, due; — that which is owing, a due.
adj. = ćahūṅ, q.v.

ety: ćah˚ = ćaʼu+ūṅ = Prk. ऊहं = S. नाम् aff. of the gen. plur.
def: adj. Four; the four, all four ( = ćāroṅ)
subs: (S. ćatuś+ćakra), adv. = ćahuṅ-dis, or disi, or diś, or diśā, or diśi (S. ćatur+diśam) = ćahuṅ-or, adv. On all four sides, all around, in all directions or quarters, circumjacently ( = ćāroṅ-or).

def: v.n. = ćauṅkna, q.v.
s.m. (Old H.) = ćauhān, q.v.
s.m.(?) Land twice tilled.
s.m. A log or billet (of wood), a piece of split wood (for burning).
s.f. A small piece of split wood, a chip, shaving; saw-dust.
